result.markdown:
---
title: "All the R you need for STAT 220 - formula"
author: "Professor McNamara"
date: "Fall 2020"
output: html_document
---




## Packages

Loading two packages for formula based basic statistics


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
library(mosaic)
library(ggformula)
```
:::


## Reading in data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
GSS <- read.csv("GSS_clean.csv")
```
:::


## Plotting


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# one categorical
gf_bar(~marital_status, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-1.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# two categorical
gf_bar(~marital_status, fill = ~born_in_us, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-2.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
gf_bar(~marital_status, fill = ~born_in_us, data = GSS, position = "dodge")
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-3.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# one numeric
gf_histogram(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-4.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
gf_boxplot(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-5.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# one numeric, one categorical
gf_boxplot(highest_year_of_school_completed ~ labor_force_status, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-6.png){width=672}
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# two numeric
gf_point(highest_year_of_school_completed ~ highest_year_school_completed_spouse,
  data = GSS
)
```

::: {.cell-output-display}
![](all_the_R_you_need_formula_files/figure-html/unnamed-chunk-3-7.png){width=672}
:::
:::


## Summary statistics


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# one categorical
tally(~marital_status, data = GSS, format = "proportion")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
marital_status
     Divorced       Married Never married     Separated       Widowed 
 0.1716354344  0.4250425894  0.2853492334  0.0319420784  0.0851788756 
         <NA> 
 0.0008517888 
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# two categorical
tally(~ marital_status | general_happiness, data = GSS, format = "proportion")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
               general_happiness
marital_status  Not too happy Pretty happy   Very happy         <NA>
  Divorced       0.2500000000 0.1851568477 0.1098430813 0.0000000000
  Married        0.1815476190 0.3856159143 0.6162624822 0.2500000000
  Never married  0.4017857143 0.3129303749 0.1768901569 0.5000000000
  Separated      0.0565476190 0.0306044376 0.0213980029 0.2500000000
  Widowed        0.1101190476 0.0849273145 0.0741797432 0.0000000000
  <NA>           0.0000000000 0.0007651109 0.0014265335 0.0000000000
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# one numeric
mean(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS, na.rm = TRUE)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 13.73177
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
options(na.rm = TRUE) # options
median(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 14
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
sd(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 2.974313
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
range(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1]  0 20
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
IQR(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 4
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
fivenum(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1]  0 12 14 16 20
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
favstats(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS) # favorite statistics
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
 min Q1 median Q3 max     mean       sd    n missing
   0 12     14 16  20 13.73177 2.974313 2345       3
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
cor(highest_year_of_school_completed ~ highest_year_school_completed_spouse,
  data = GSS, use = "complete.obs"
) # correlation
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 0.5955573
```
:::
:::


## Working with data


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# filtering out NA values
GSS_prop <- filter(
  GSS,
  !is.na(should_marijuana_be_made_legal),
  !is.na(self_emp_or_works_for_somebody)
)
```
:::


## Modeling and inference


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
# linear model
m1 <- lm(highest_year_of_school_completed ~ highest_year_school_completed_spouse,
  data = GSS
)
summary(m1)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

Call:
lm(formula = highest_year_of_school_completed ~ highest_year_school_completed_spouse, 
    data = GSS)

Residuals:
     Min       1Q   Median       3Q      Max 
-16.5400  -1.3519   0.0242   1.4600   7.0242 

Coefficients:
                                     Estimate Std. Error t value Pr(>|t|)    
(Intercept)                           5.84740    0.36316   16.10   <2e-16 ***
highest_year_school_completed_spouse  0.59403    0.02553   23.27   <2e-16 ***
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1

Residual standard error: 2.462 on 985 degrees of freedom
  (1361 observations deleted due to missingness)
Multiple R-squared:  0.3547,	Adjusted R-squared:  0.354 
F-statistic: 541.4 on 1 and 985 DF,  p-value: < 2.2e-16
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
rsquared(m1)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 0.3546884
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
predict(m1,
  newdata = data.frame(highest_year_school_completed_spouse = 11),
  interval = "confidence"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
       fit      lwr      upr
1 12.38178 12.17059 12.59297
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
predict(m1,
  newdata = data.frame(highest_year_school_completed_spouse = 11),
  interval = "prediction"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
       fit      lwr      upr
1 12.38178 7.546498 17.21706
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# bootstrapping
bootstrap_sample <- do(1) * resample(GSS)
boot <- do(1000) * mean(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = resample(GSS))
confint(boot)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
  name    lower    upper level     method estimate
1 mean 13.62109 13.85124  0.95 percentile 13.73177
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# one proportion
prop.test(~self_emp_or_works_for_somebody,
  data = GSS, success = "Self-employed", p = 0.1, alternative = "less"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

	1-sample proportions test with continuity correction

data:  GSS$self_emp_or_works_for_somebody  [with success = Self-employed]
X-squared = 0.1433, df = 1, p-value = 0.6475
alternative hypothesis: true p is less than 0.1
95 percent confidence interval:
 0.0000000 0.1138167
sample estimates:
        p 
0.1026095 
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# two proportions
prop.test(should_marijuana_be_made_legal ~ self_emp_or_works_for_somebody,
  data = GSS_prop, success = "Legal"
)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

	2-sample test for equality of proportions with continuity correction

data:  tally(should_marijuana_be_made_legal ~ self_emp_or_works_for_somebody)
X-squared = 0.87754, df = 1, p-value = 0.3489
alternative hypothesis: two.sided
95 percent confidence interval:
 -0.04100264  0.12791902
sample estimates:
   prop 1    prop 2 
0.6901408 0.6466827 
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# one mean
t.test(~highest_year_of_school_completed, data = GSS, mu = 12, alternative = "greater")
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

	One Sample t-test

data:  highest_year_of_school_completed
t = 28.195, df = 2344, p-value < 2.2e-16
alternative hypothesis: true mean is greater than 12
95 percent confidence interval:
 13.6307     Inf
sample estimates:
mean of x 
 13.73177 
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# two means
t.test(highest_year_of_school_completed ~ born_in_us, data = GSS)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

	Welch Two Sample t-test

data:  highest_year_of_school_completed by born_in_us
t = -4.8468, df = 337.79, p-value = 1.916e-06
alternative hypothesis: true difference in means between group No and group Yes is not equal to 0
95 percent confidence interval:
 -1.7012965 -0.7190416
sample estimates:
 mean in group No mean in group Yes 
         12.67774          13.88791 
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# ANOVA
a1 <- aov(highest_year_of_school_completed ~ labor_force_status, data = GSS)
summary(a1)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
                     Df Sum Sq Mean Sq F value   Pr(>F)    
labor_force_status    7    748  106.86   12.49 8.47e-16 ***
Residuals          2336  19985    8.56                     
---
Signif. codes:  0 '***' 0.001 '**' 0.01 '*' 0.05 '.' 0.1 ' ' 1
4 observations deleted due to missingness
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
TukeyHSD(a1, conf.level = 0.90)
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
  Tukey multiple comparisons of means
    90% family-wise confidence level

Fit: aov(formula = highest_year_of_school_completed ~ labor_force_status, data = GSS)

$labor_force_status
                                        diff        lwr         upr     p adj
Other-Keeping house               -0.5946970 -1.8803606  0.69096668 0.9039503
Retired-Keeping house              0.9186925  0.2687913  1.56859375 0.0022026
School-Keeping house               1.3512907  0.3067924  2.39578898 0.0078444
Temp not working-Keeping house     2.2315609  0.9975413  3.46558049 0.0000145
Unempl, laid off-Keeping house     0.1969697 -0.8334595  1.22739886 0.9994939
Working fulltime-Keeping house     1.4766108  0.9003947  2.05282685 0.0000000
Working parttime-Keeping house     1.0535588  0.3254033  1.78171434 0.0015139

Temp not working-Other             2.8262579  1.2049750  4.44754075 0.0000361
Unempl, laid off-Other             0.7916667 -0.6805895  2.26392279 0.8099193

Working parttime-Other             1.6482558  0.3692076  2.92730401 0.0082537

Temp not working-Retired           1.3128683  0.1304984  2.49523834 0.0424610
Unempl, laid off-Retired          -0.7217228 -1.6896994  0.24625374 0.4320893
Working fulltime-Retired           0.5579182  0.1027046  1.01313180 0.0152433
Working parttime-Retired           0.1348663 -0.5018478  0.77158041 0.9990087
Temp not working-School            0.8802702 -0.5572985  2.31783895 0.6851008
Unempl, laid off-School           -1.1543210 -2.4214373  0.11279534 0.1815282
Working fulltime-School            0.1253201 -0.8105349  1.06117504 0.9999535
Working parttime-School           -0.2977318 -1.3340764  0.73861267 0.9931893
Unempl, laid off-Temp not working -2.0345912 -3.4619704 -0.60721197 0.0019303

Working parttime-Temp not working -1.1780020 -2.4051278  0.04912371 0.1321690

Working parttime-Working fulltime -0.4230519 -0.9843524  0.13824857 0.4172636
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
# Chi-square
chisq.test(tally(marital_status ~ general_happiness, data = GSS)) # independence
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

	Pearson's Chi-squared test

data:  tally(marital_status ~ general_happiness, data = GSS)
X-squared = 210.13, df = 15, p-value < 2.2e-16
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
chisq.test(tally(~general_happiness, data = GSS), p = c(0.14, 0.55, 0.30, 0.01)) # GoF
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```

	Chi-squared test for given probabilities

data:  tally(~general_happiness, data = GSS)
X-squared = 16.528, df = 3, p-value = 0.0008838
```
:::
:::


## Probability


::: {.cell}

```{.r .cell-code}
pnorm(1.96, lower.tail = FALSE) # normal distribution function
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 0.0249979
```
:::

```{.r .cell-code}
qnorm(0.25, mean = 100, sd = 5) # normal quantile function
```

::: {.cell-output .cell-output-stdout}
```
[1] 96.62755
```
:::
:::
